stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta's Llama 3 8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML domains including transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML community.
The model is associated with the 'transformers' and 'llama' ecosystem, indicating relevance to open-source LLMs.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom announce chip designed for LLM inference at scale
stories.author: Samuel Axon
bullet_points: OpenAI and Broadcom are collaborating on a specialized chip designed for large language model (LLM) inference.
The announcement highlights the intensifying 'silicon race' driven by massive demand for AI processing power.
This development signals a critical industry shift toward custom hardware solutions to handle LLM scaling.

stories.title: Real-Time Voice AI Hears but Does Not Listen
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Leading real-time voice AI systems (GPT, Gemini, Qwen) fail to interpret vocal delivery cues.
The research demonstrates that current models prioritize semantic content (words) over prosodic information (tone/delivery).
This failure leads to critical errors in simulated scenarios, such as mishandling distressed callers or financial transactions.

stories.title: OpenAI unveils its first custom chip, built by Broadcom
stories.author: jamdesk
bullet_points: OpenAI is developing its first custom AI inference chip, named 'Jalapeno', in partnership with Broadcom.
This move signifies a major industry trend toward specialized, in-house hardware for optimizing AI workloads.
The development aims to improve the efficiency and performance of OpenAI's AI models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The tags suggest relevance to modern transformer architectures and conversational AI applications.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function in language tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing development and optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting external communication tools to private, local AI capabilities.

stories.title: IBM claims world’s first sub-1 nanometer chip technology
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: IBM has announced a claim regarding the world's first sub-1 nanometer chip technology.
The technology utilizes 'nanostack transistors' as its core innovation.
Potential benefits include significant boosts to chip performance or energy efficiency.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of creating fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to long-horizon and irreversible actions.
It proposes that Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training inherently contains the necessary components for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for the complex and often infeasible process of training dedicated reward models for agentic LLMs.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the vulnerability of current AI safety measures, noting that controls placed within an agent's own runtime (like prompts or filters) are susceptible to manipulation.
It introduces the concept of 'escapable AI systems,' which are AI agents capable of influencing or bypassing their own internal safety mechanisms.
The research proposes a new framework, the 'Unfireable Safety Kernel,' to implement execution-time AI alignment controls outside the agent's direct address space.

stories.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
stories.author: Sharon Li
bullet_points: The paper addresses the difficulty of creating fine-grained reward models for LLM agents due to complex, long-horizon interactions.
It proposes that Reinforcement Learning (RL) post-training inherently contains the necessary components for effective step-level scoring.
This approach potentially eliminates the need for expensive and difficult dedicated reward model training in agentic LLM systems.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical gap in current MLLM evaluation: the lack of testing for order sensitivity in multimodal inputs.
They introduce 'Facet-Probe,' a five-facet audit designed to rigorously test MLLMs across various ordering scenarios (e.g., evidence chunk, document rank).
The methodology uses a Bayesian item-response model to effectively separate inherent model bias from noise introduced by input ordering.

stories.title: The Unfireable Safety Kernel: Execution-Time AI Alignment for AI Agents and Other Escapable AI Systems
stories.author: Łukasz Chmiel
bullet_points: The paper addresses the vulnerability of AI agents whose controls (like prompts and filters) reside within their own runtime environment.
It introduces the concept of 'escapable AI systems,' where internal controls are susceptible to external inputs.
The research proposes a new approach—an 'Unfireable Safety Kernel'—for execution-time AI alignment, moving controls outside the agent's address space.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: Investigates the catastrophic collapse observed in multi-step tool-use Reinforcement Learning (RL) for LLMs.
Identifies that RL alone often leads to instability and performance degradation in complex agentic tasks.
Proposes that incorporating supervisory signals can resolve these failures, which are linked to unexpected probability spikes in control tokens.

stories.title: Same Evidence, Different Answer: Auditing Order Sensitivity in Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Ehsan Adeli
bullet_points: Addresses a critical gap in current MLLM evaluation: the failure to test for order sensitivity in multimodal inputs.
Introduces 'Facet-Probe,' a comprehensive five-facet auditing framework to rigorously test MLLM reliability.
Employs a Bayesian item-response model to distinguish true model bias from noise introduced by input ordering.

stories.title: Detect, Unlearn, Restore: Defending Text Summarization Models Against Data Poisoning
stories.author: Shirin Nilizadeh
bullet_points: Addresses the critical security threat of data poisoning during the fine-tuning of LLMs for text summarization.
Highlights that poisoning attacks can induce persistent, harmful summarization failures while bypassing standard evaluation metrics.
Introduces a unified post-hoc defense framework designed for detecting and remediating these adversarial attacks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents can run locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models offline.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Leica’s $6,690 SL3-P pairs 44-megapixel stills with 8K video
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Leica has released the new SL3-P camera, priced at $6,690.
The camera combines 44-megapixel still capabilities with 8K video recording.
It integrates features from both the SL3 and SL3-S models, while notably omitting the red dot viewfinder.

stories.title: Ford had to hire back former engineers to fix mistakes made by its automated systems
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Ford is publicly addressing issues with its automated production and design systems.
The company had to rehire former engineers to correct flaws in its automated processes.
This admission comes as Ford celebrates a high ranking in JD Power's quality survey.

stories.title: Facebook’s Creator Studio has been revived as an AI companion app
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Meta is reviving and reimagining the Facebook Creator Studio as a standalone AI companion app.
The primary goal of the new AI Creator Assistant is to help creators connect with audiences and guide them on growth strategies within Facebook.
This represents Meta's continued integration of AI tools directly into its creator ecosystem.

stories.title: One-two punch delivered in global operation disrupts cybercrime "assembly line"
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Global operation named 'Operation Endgame' was launched to combat cybercrime.
The operation successfully disrupted two widely utilized tools within the cybercrime infrastructure.
The focus is on disrupting the 'assembly line' used by cybercriminals.

stories.title: FCC plans ID mandate that could block anonymous use of prepaid burner phones
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC is planning an ID mandate for prepaid burner phones.
This mandate could restrict the anonymous use of these phones.
Privacy advocates and domestic violence groups oppose the requirement.

stories.title: Learning Action Priors for Cross-embodiment Robot Manipulation
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which often lack explicit motion priors.
Existing VLA models rely on VLM backbones but force the action module to learn physical motion from scratch.
The research proposes learning action priors to simplify the optimization process, especially for cross-embodiment robot manipulation.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using one model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach can reduce output diversity and flatten pass@k curves.
The authors attribute this issue to compounding biases inherent in using sampled demonstrations within the self-distillation design.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'model forensics' as a method to distinguish between concerning AI behavior and benign confusion.
It addresses the limitation of prior work, which only focused on detecting concerning behavior without proving misalignment.
A baseline two-step protocol is proposed for investigating whether an AI's action stems from malign intent.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a language model spontaneously forgets a learned rule during pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal (e.g., forgetting pronoun-gender rules) occurs without any corresponding change in the model's loss curve.
This suggests the training corpus itself dictates which learned rules are retained by the model, challenging current understanding of stable learning.

stories.title: TriViewBench: Controlled Complexity Scaling for Multi-View Structural Reasoning in MLLMs
stories.author: Lan-Zhe Guo
bullet_points: Introduces TriViewBench, a new benchmark designed to test Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) on structural reasoning.
The benchmark uses synthetic 3D scenes with controlled parameters like object count and occlusion to manage complexity.
It addresses a gap in current research by systematically evaluating MLLM scalability beyond standard visual QA tasks.

stories.title: Can Trustless Agents Be Trusted? An Empirical Study of the ERC-8004 Decentralized AI Agent Ecosystem
stories.author: Zhipeng Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical trust challenge in autonomous AI agent ecosystems as they cross organizational boundaries.
Introduces ERC-8004, a permissionless trust layer for AI agent economies utilizing on-chain registries for Identity, Reputation, and Validation.
The study aims to empirically validate the effectiveness of this rapidly adopted protocol.

stories.title: On-Policy Self-Distillation with Sampled Demonstrations Reduces Output Diversity
stories.author: Aaron Courville
bullet_points: The paper introduces On-Policy Self-Distillation, a method achieving high pass@1 accuracy by using a single model as both teacher and student.
A key finding is that this self-distillation approach can reduce output diversity and flatten pass@k curves, indicating a potential hidden cost.
The authors attribute this issue to compounding biases inherent in the design of self-distillation when using sampled demonstrations.

stories.title: Model Forensics: Investigating Whether Concerning Behavior Reflects Misalignment
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of simply detecting concerning AI behavior, noting that behavior alone doesn't prove misalignment.
It introduces 'model forensics' as a new approach to investigate whether concerning actions stem from malign intent rather than benign causes like confusion.
A baseline protocol for model forensics is proposed, involving an iterative two-step investigation process.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal occurs without any corresponding change in the model's loss curve.
The mechanism suggests the training corpus itself dictates which learned linguistic rules are retained by the model.

stories.title: FedReLa: Imbalanced Federated Learning via Re-Labeling
stories.author: Liuhua Peng
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of class imbalance in Federated Learning (FL), a critical issue for decentralized training.
Proposes 'FedReLa,' a method utilizing re-labeling to mitigate performance degradation caused by data heterogeneity.
Highlights the difficulty of global class distribution agnosticism when dealing with severe class absence across distributed clients.

stories.title: When Certainty Is an Artifact: Keyword Lexicon Blindness and the (Mis)Measurement of Rhetorical Stance
stories.author: Bo Chen
bullet_points: The research questions whether statistically significant findings in computational social science can be measurement artifacts.
It demonstrates that a strong correlation between negative affect and emphatic certainty was found using keyword-based scoring.
Replacing keyword counting with LLM-based semantic classification significantly changes the measurement outcome, suggesting lexicon blindness.

stories.title: Natural Ungrokking: Asymmetric Control of Which Rules Survive Pretraining
stories.author: Diya Sreedhar
bullet_points: Introduces 'natural ungrokking,' a phenomenon where a small language model spontaneously forgets learned rules during pretraining.
The study demonstrates this reversal (e.g., forgetting pronoun-gender rules) occurs without any detectable change in the model's loss curve.
This suggests an emergent, corpus-driven mechanism controlling which learned linguistic rules are retained by the model.

stories.title: How Robust is OCR-Reasoning? Evaluating OCR-Reasoning Robustness of Vision-Language Models under Visual Perturbations
stories.author: Yi Chang
bullet_points: The study addresses the lack of understanding regarding the robustness of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) when dealing with visual degradation in OCR-based tasks.
Visual corruption poses a critical threat to OCR reasoning, as it can cause both OCR errors and structural distortions.
The authors introduce 'OCR-Robust,' a new benchmark specifically designed to systematically evaluate this robustness issue.

stories.title: Why Multi-Step Tool-Use Reinforcement Learning Collapses and How Supervisory Signals Fix It
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The paper addresses instability and performance collapse in LLM agentic reinforcement learning (RL) when using tools.
Catastrophic collapse in tool-use tasks is linked to unexpected probability spikes in specific control tokens.
The research proposes that incorporating supervisory signals can effectively resolve these RL-based tool-use failures.

stories.title: The Tatoxa System for Text Detoxification in Low-Resource Languages: The Case of Tatar
stories.author: Alexander Panchenko
bullet_points: Introduces 'Tatoxa,' a novel system for text detoxification specifically designed for the Tatar language.
Addresses the critical gap in research regarding abusive content detection in low-resource languages.
Demonstrates that the proposed system outperforms existing open-source and commercial LLMs in the task.

stories.title: Dziri Voicebot: An End-to-End Low-Resource Speech-to-Speech Conversational System for Algerian Dialect
stories.author: Asma Kemmoum
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of low-resource NLP by developing a speech-to-speech system for Algerian Dialect.
Tackles specific linguistic hurdles of the dialect, including non-standard orthography and code-switching with French.
Contributes to making AI/ML technologies more applicable to dialectal and underserved language settings.

stories.title: Countries are competing to see which can carry out mass surveillance the best
stories.author: Cider9986
bullet_points: The article discusses a geopolitical trend where nations are competing in surveillance capabilities.
The focus is on the development and deployment of mass surveillance technologies by governments.
This topic touches upon significant privacy and civil liberties concerns in the modern digital age.

stories.title: Cognitive Pong: An Open Source Arena Where AI Agents Compete, Learn, and Train
stories.author: bclonan
bullet_points: Introduces 'Cognitive Pong,' an open-source platform for competitive AI agent training.
The core hypothesis is that structured competition can enhance AI reasoning and data quality.
The project aims to generate better training data and ultimately improve AI models.

stories.title: AI System Design Interview Questions: ChatGPT, RAG, LLM Inference, and Agents
stories.author: arslan_ah
bullet_points: The focus of system design interviews is shifting towards modern AI/ML concepts.
Key topics covered include ChatGPT, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and LLM Inference.
The article addresses the evolution of system design questions beyond traditional examples like 'Design Twitter'.
